forecasts.reasoning: The government has explicitly stated (as recently as May 6, 2025) that it will not initiate an independence referendum itself, leaving it entirely to a citizen-led petition process under the newly introduced Bill 54. The process requires gathering ~177,000 signatures over a 120-day period. Because Bill 54 is still moving through the legislature and the 120-day signature collection window extends well past the July 1, 2025 deadline, it is virtually impossible for the petition to conclude, be verified, and have a definitive date officially scheduled by July 1. An official scheduling usually requires an Order in Council or a similarly formal binding decree, which will not occur before the triggering condition (the petition) is met. Given the tight 54-day window to July 1, 2025, the probability is extremely low.

Starlink was officially granted a 10-year license in Lesotho on April 14, 2025. The resolution date of July 1, 2025, allows ~78 days (2.5 months) for the service to launch and the map to update to 'Available'. Recent Starlink launches in Africa range from taking just days (like the DRC in early May 2025) or <1 month (Eswatini), up to 3-4 months (Botswana and Zimbabwe). The rapid 2-month processing time by the Lesotho Communications Authority shows strong regulatory enthusiasm. While there is minor pushback from a local rights group ('Section 2') demanding 30% local equity, the official license has already been issued. Given Starlink's accelerated deployment speed recently, 2.5 months is highly likely to be enough time to go live, bringing the probability to around 70%.

The question asks whether an FSIS recall issued on March 28, 2025, will be marked as "Closed" before July 1, 2025. This allows a window of about 94 days (just over 3 months) for the recall to be completed, verified by FSIS, and successfully updated on the agency's website. The base rate for official closure of federal food recalls (whether FDA or USDA) is typically longer than 3 months, often taking 6-12 months or more. This is because closing a recall requires the company to complete product recovery or destruction, provide documentation, and for the FSIS to conduct and finish its effectiveness checks, followed by an administrative review and website update. 

While the recall involves liquid egg products primarily going to foodservice or institutional customers (which makes tracking and recovery somewhat faster than direct-to-consumer retail), the volume is substantial (over 212,000 lbs). Given standard governmental processing times and the amount of product involved, a ~3-month turnaround to official 'Closed' status is quite aggressive. Given these factors, the base rate suggests it is relatively unlikely to close within this short timeframe.

The US and China are currently in a diplomatic freeze over a severe tariff escalation initiated by Trump in April 2025. Although earlier in March there were discussions of a "birthday summit" in June, the April tariffs seem to have derailed this. The Trump administration privately demanded Xi call Trump and publicly claimed a call occurred, which China angrily and publicly denied on April 28, explicitly stating they are "not engaged in consultations or negotiations." Such diplomatic freezes typically last several weeks to months, often only resolving at multilateral summits later in the year. While a "birthday call" around June 14 is possible, Xi is highly unlikely to initiate it as it would appear as capitulating to Trump's public demands for a call. Given the deep standoff and China's explicit denial of ongoing negotiations, the probability of mutually acknowledged call between May 12 and June 15 is relatively low, around 31%.

In March 2025, mass anti-government protests erupted in Turkey over the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu. Multiple credible news outlets, including Reuters, CBS, and DW, reported protest sizes ranging from 'tens of thousands' to 'hundreds of thousands' in locations such as Istanbul's Saraçhane Square. The Global Protest Tracker maintains a comprehensive database of such anti-government demonstrations globally. Given the tracker's methodology and the immense national and international visibility of these specific protests (along with their clear anti-government nature and size vastly exceeding the 10,000-participant threshold), it is almost certain the tracker will include this event in a future update prior to the July 2025 resolution date. Assuming the question launched prior to March 2025 (as is typical for such end-of-quarter or year-ahead forecasts), this event firmly satisfies the resolution criteria.
